items.title: Keep `main()` thin; delegate to importable functions
items.avoid: `main()` opening files, running the algorithm, talking to APIs, and printing — all parsing/IO/logic in one place.
items.prefer: `main()` parses argv and dispatches; real work lives in `run()` / library functions.
items.why: A thin CLI is testable (call `main([...])` directly), reusable (other code can import the same logic), and replaceable (swap to HTTP/GUI without rewriting business code).
items.example:
```python
def main(argv=None) -> int:
    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
    return run(args)   # all real work here
```

items.title: Results to stdout; messages to stderr
items.avoid: Printing progress, warnings, or error text to stdout — breaks pipes (`mycli list | grep foo`).
items.prefer: Use `print(result)` for results; `print('done', file=sys.stderr)` or `logging` for everything else.
items.why: The only way scripting works reliably — pipes consume stdout, terminals show stderr; mixing them poisons both.
items.example:
```python
print(result_json)                       # stdout: pipeable
print('processed 17 files', file=sys.stderr)  # stderr: visible, not in pipe
```

items.title: Return explicit integer exit codes
items.avoid: Returning `None` from `main` (becomes implicit `0`) or letting unhandled exceptions decide the exit code.
items.prefer: Return `0` on success, specific non-zero codes for distinct failures; wrap with `sys.exit(main())`.
items.why: Shells and CI branch on exit codes — they're the CLI's API for success/failure with precision.

items.title: Support `-` for stdin where it makes sense
items.avoid: Always requiring a file path, forcing users to write `mycli /dev/stdin` or temp files.
items.prefer: Treat `-` as stdin by convention: `cat doc.txt | mycli chunk -`.
items.why: Makes the CLI a first-class Unix citizen — composes naturally with pipes, `xargs`, and process substitution.
items.example:
```python
text = sys.stdin.read() if path == '-' else Path(path).read_text()
```

items.title: Register the CLI as an installed command
items.avoid: Asking users to invoke `python -m mypkg.cli` (works, but ugly and discoverable only if they know the package layout).
items.prefer: Add `[project.scripts] mycli = 'mypkg.cli:main'`; pip generates a wrapper script on PATH cross-platform.
items.why: Users type `mycli`, not `python -m mypkg.cli`. Better UX, fewer support questions, identical on Linux/macOS/Windows.

items.title: Write at least one end-to-end CLI test
items.avoid: Testing only the underlying library while assuming the CLI glue 'just works'.
items.prefer: Call `main(['cmd', '--flag', 'value'])` in-process and assert on return value + captured stdout/stderr via `capsys`.
items.why: Catches argv parsing regressions, default changes, and output format breakage before users see them.
items.example:
```python
def test_search(capsys):
    assert main(['search', 'query', '-k', '3']) == 0
    assert 'result' in capsys.readouterr().out
```
